audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for wilderness testing, Spirit-filled return, Nazareth synagogue manifesto from Isaiah, rejection, Capernaum authority, unclean spirit release, Simon's mother-in-law, healing summary, and proclamation mission.

verses.restored: Jesus, full of holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan and was led in the Spirit in the wilderness
forty days, being tested by the adversarial one. He ate nothing in those days, and when they were completed, he was hungry.
The adversarial one said to him, If you are Son of the Divine, speak to this stone so that it may become bread.
Jesus answered him, It has been written: A human does not live by bread alone.
Leading him up, he showed him all the reigns of the inhabited world in a moment of time.
The adversarial one said to him, I will give you all this authority and their glory, because it has been handed over to me, and I give it to whomever I wish.
Therefore, if you bow/prostrate before me, it will all be yours.
Jesus answered him, It has been written: You shall bow/prostrate to the Lord your Divine, and him alone shall you serve.
He led him to Jerusalem and stood him on the pinnacle of the temple and said to him, If you are Son of the Divine, throw yourself down from here.
For it has been written: He will command his messengers concerning you, to guard you,
and, On their hands they will lift you up, lest you strike your foot against a stone.
Jesus answered him, It has been said: You shall not test the Lord your Divine.
Having completed every test, the adversarial one departed from him until an opportune time.
Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee, and a report about him went out through the whole surrounding region.
He taught in their synagogues, being glorified by all.
He came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up. According to his custom, he entered the synagogue on the Sabbath day and stood up to read.
The scroll of the prophet Isaiah was given to him. Unrolling the scroll, he found the place where it was written:

Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he anointed me to announce good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim release to captives and recovery of sight to the blind, to send away the oppressed in release,
to proclaim the favorable year of the Lord.
Rolling up the scroll, giving it back to the attendant, he sat down. The eyes of all in the synagogue were fixed on him.
He began to say to them, Today this Scripture has been fulfilled in your ears.
All were bearing witness to him and marveling at the words of favor coming from his mouth, and they said, Is this not Joseph's son?
He said to them, Surely you will say to me this parable: Physician, heal yourself. Whatever we heard happened in Capernaum, do here also in your homeland.
He said, Amen, I say to you: no prophet is accepted in the prophet's homeland.
In truth I say to you, there were many widows in Israel in the days of Elijah, when heaven was shut three years and six months and a great famine came over all the land.
Yet Elijah was sent to none of them except to Zarephath in Sidon, to a widow woman.
There were many with skin disease in Israel in the time of Elisha the prophet, and none of them was cleansed except Naaman the Syrian.
All in the synagogue were filled with rage when they heard these things.
They rose up, drove him out of the city, and led him to the brow of the hill on which their city had been built, to throw him down.
But he passed through the middle of them and went away.
He went down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and was teaching them on the Sabbaths.
They were struck with wonder at his teaching, because his word was with authority.
In the synagogue there was a man having a spirit of an unclean force, and he cried out with a great voice,
Ah! What to us and to you, Jesus Nazarene? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are: the Holy One of the Divine.
Jesus rebuked it, saying, Be silent and come out from him. Throwing him into the middle, the troubling spirit came out from him, harming him nothing.
Amazement came upon all, and they spoke with one another, saying, What is this word? For with authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and they come out.
A report about him went out into every place of the surrounding region.
Rising from the synagogue, he entered Simon's house. Simon's mother-in-law was held by a great fever, and they asked him concerning her.
Standing over her, he rebuked the fever, and it left her. Immediately she rose and served them.
When the sun was setting, all who had any sick with various diseases brought them to him. Placing hands on each one, he healed them.
Unclean spirits also came out from many, crying out and saying, You are the Son of the Divine. Rebuking them, he did not allow them to speak, because they knew him to be the Messiah.
When day came, he went out and traveled to a deserted place. The crowds were seeking him and came to him, and they were trying to hold him back so he would not go away from them.
But he said to them, It is necessary for me to announce the good news of the reign of the Divine to the other cities also, because for this I was sent.
He was proclaiming in the synagogues of Judea.
